title: aquilegia vulgaris var. nemoralis
intro: Aquilegia vulgaris var. nemoralis, also known as the woodland columbine, is a variety of the wild columbine plant that is native to Europe and Western Asia. This perennial plant has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties, which can help to soothe wounds, reduce fever, and alleviate respiratory issues. The health benefits of Aquilegia vulgaris var. nemoralis include its ability to reduce inflammation, improve digestion, and boost the immune system, making it a popular herbal remedy for various health conditions. Herbal preparations of this plant include teas, infusions, and tinctures, which can be used to treat a range of ailments, from colds and coughs to skin conditions and digestive issues.
